commit.message:
Fix expander arrow CSS — cursor, pointer-events, SVG size, markers

The arrow/toggle on st.expander (Geo Refinement, IO Queue) was
unresponsive on Streamlit Cloud. Root cause: global font-family
!important on all elements + missing cursor/pointer-events rules
broke the clickable summary element.

Fixes:
- summary { cursor: pointer } — ensures clickable cursor
- summary svg { pointer-events: none; min-width/height: 16px }
- details { pointer-events: auto } — ensures container is clickable
- ::marker, ::-webkit-details-marker { display: block } — shows arrow

feat: Deep Agentic integration, Notepad app, and Proactive AURA intelligence

  • Notepad application with localStorage, cloud upload, and real file downloads

⚡ Edge AI Optimization:

🎯 Proactive Intelligence:
  • Monitor window count and trigger proactive alerts when >10 windows open
  • AURA speaks: 'Sir, the system feels a bit heavy. Shall I close background apps?'
  • Elegant UI notification with 4-second timeout
  • Auto-hide if no user response within timeout window
  • 30-second cooldown between alerts to prevent spam

  • artifacts/kali-portfolio/src/lib/ProactiveMonitor.ts - Window monitoring & alerts
  • artifacts/kali-portfolio/src/components/ProactiveNotification.tsx - Alert UI
  • Updated Desktop.tsx - Notepad integration + monitor initialization
  • Updated AuraService.ts - Notepad in voice command mapper

✨ Zero Breaking Changes:
  • No new dependencies
  • Surgical code additions (~400 lines)
  • Follows existing MONIX OS patterns
  • Integrated with existing AURA pipeline

commit.message:
feat: AdaptiveConcurrency の増速制御を成功カウントから時間ベース（秒数）に変更 (#133)

* feat: AdaptiveConcurrency の増速制御を成功カウントから時間ベースに変更

- SuccessThreshold（成功カウント）→ IncreaseIntervalSec（秒数）に変更
- 小ファイル多数移行時の過敏な増速を防止
- Retry-After は加算方式: now + retryAfter + increaseIntervalSec
- デフォルト 30 秒、0 指定で即時増速可能（テスト向け）
- SetIncreaseAvailableNow() 内部メソッドをテスト補助用に追加
- InitialDegree（ソフトスタート）機能含む（SettingsPage UI 対応済み）

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: PRレビュー指摘対応（AdaptiveConcurrency 時間ベース制御の修正）

- AdaptiveConcurrencyController: NotifyRateLimit で DecreaseTriggerCount>1 の場合も
  pendingDecreases 加算時点で _increaseAvailableAfterTicks を更新し、減速発火前の
  即時増速を防止
- AdaptiveConcurrencyController: NotifySuccess の XML コメントを IncreaseIntervalSec
  ベースの説明に更新（旧 SuccessThreshold 記述を修正）
- SharePointMigrationPipeline: Phase C の MaxDegreeOfParallelism を
  min(MaxParallelFolderCreations, controller.MaxDegree) でキャップし、
  MaxParallelFolderCreations 設定が無視されるバグを修正
- ConfigurationService: adaptiveConcurrency.sharepoint キーが存在しない場合に
  default プロファイルへフォールバックし、設定保存で enabled=false が書き込まれて
  アダプティブ制御が無効化されるバグを修正
- SettingsPage: AdaptiveConcurrencyInitialDegree の 0〜256 範囲バリデーションを追加
- AdaptiveConcurrencyControllerTests: コメント誤字「合記」→「合計」を修正

Co-Authored-By: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: PRレビュー第2回指摘対応（AdaptiveConcurrency / Phase C）

- AdaptiveConcurrencyController: _increaseAvailableAfterTicks の更新を
  if (_current > _min) ガード外に移動し、MinDegree 張り付き時にも
  429/503 直後の NotifySuccess による即時増速を防止
- SharePointMigrationPipeline: Phase C の current_parallelism 記録を
  Math.Min(maxDegree, controller?.CurrentDegree ?? maxDegree) に統一し、
  MaxParallelFolderCreations キャップを超えた値がダッシュボードに表示されないよう修正
- AdaptiveConcurrencyControllerTests: ソフトスタートテストのコメント「SetIncreaseAvailableNow 後に」を削除
  （increaseIntervalSec=0 のため不要、実装と不整合だった）

fix: channel-based exfil logger — eliminate concurrent write race

Replace shared *os.File with a buffered channel (cap 256) owned by a
single writer goroutine:

- Multiple handleConn goroutines call writeExfilLog concurrently;
  concurrent WriteString on the same *os.File is not safe and could
  produce interleaved or lost log entries.

- The writer goroutine is the sole owner of the file handle; it reads
  from exfilCh and writes entries sequentially, surfacing any write
  errors to stderr rather than silently discarding them.

- On SIGTERM/SIGINT the accept loop exits, exfilCh is closed, and
  writerDone.Wait() ensures all queued detection events are flushed
  before the process exits.

- writeExfilLog uses a non-blocking select/default so a full channel
  (e.g. during an unexpected burst) logs a warning to stderr rather
  than blocking the ICAP handler goroutine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: lazy-init india-pincode + serverExternalPackages to fix build

The `india-pincode` package reads `data/pincodes.json.gz` from disk at
init time. When `getIndiaPincode()` was called at module scope, Next.js
Turbopack evaluated it during `next build` page data collection, causing
"data file not found" because the data file wasn't in the bundled output.

Two fixes:
1. Add `serverExternalPackages: ['india-pincode']` to next.config.ts so
   the package is left as a runtime require() and can resolve its data
   file from node_modules/.
2. Make `getIndiaPincode()` lazy (called on first request, not at module
   eval time) as a defence-in-depth measure.

commit.message:
Feature: user defined Neumann BC (#2437)

This PR is motivated by the temperature equation in a ABL simulation
with diurnal cycles where the surface heat flux is always fluctuating
with time. The PR is just drafted so that people will not work
repeatedly.

The validation is performed on a heat equation
```math
s_t = \nu s_{xx}, \; x \in [0,1], \; \nu = 0.1,
```
with initial condition
```math
s(x,0) = 0,
```
and boundary conditions
```math
\mathrm{flux}(0,t) = -\nu s_x(0,t) = \sin(t),
```
```math
\mathrm{flux}(1,t) = \nu s_x(1,t) = 0.
```

And it results in

![s_animation](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/409ec65e-a88a-45aa-9b98-adf1e1b2ceae)

Also one could have two user Neumann BCs at two boundaries
```math
\mathrm{flux}(0,t) = -\nu s_x(0,t) = \sin(t),
```
```math
\mathrm{flux}(1,t) = \nu s_x(1,t) = -\sin(t).
```
And it results in

![s_animation](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c08372c5-3afb-42bb-9f2d-99b49b57fd5b)

---------

* Fix: don't include leading :: in captured alias target name

The template registry stores alias templates without the :: prefix,
so including it in the captured name causes lookup failures during
deferred alias materialization. The class alias path has no
lookup_alias_template guard, so a ::prefixed name passes the
isValid() check but fails at materialization time.

Skip the :: token without appending it to the name builder.
